original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days the base case is a corrective pullback that has now broken out of the topping pattern into an active downleg. Price has fully retraced the May-June advance, closed below the 20-day SMA, and is sitting near the lower Bollinger band with breadth at Extreme Fear and credit blowing out. The most likely path is continued downside testing toward the 7,240 nervous-zone / 50-day SMA area, with mean-reversion bounces toward 7,300-7,340 likely to be sold. A clean break of 7,240 opens a 35% conditional path toward the ~6,859 margin-call zone. Patience and defense dominate; no aggressive re-entry until a VIX capitulation-and-reversal plus price reclaim of the 20-day SMA confirms.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day SMA (~7,213) remains above the 200-day SMA (~6,872), so the mechanical bull skeleton is intact, but price (7,267) is now barely above the 50-day and has lost the 20-day SMA after an extended advance — a regime-shift signal. Trend is bullish-but-cracking, transitioning from healthy to maturing/topping.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE 19.64x (18-20x moderate buying band), but ERP at 0.55% sits in the 0.5-1.0% thin-cushion zone bordering the valuation ceiling — reduced aggression warranted.

signals.vix_regime: Elevated regime: VIX ~22.2, above the 20 risk threshold and back above its ~19 50-day MA after spiking from the mid-teens — moving-average support less reliable, forced-selling risk elevated.
signals.bollinger_position: Price near/just above the lower Bollinger band on the 1-month and 5-day views after walking the lower band lower through early June — extended to the downside, oversold watch but not yet a confirmed re-entry.
signals.middle_band_regime: Closed below the 20-day SMA / middle band; an extended-advance break of the middle band signals a regime shift, not a normal dip.
signals.intraday_close_position: Bottom third of the daily range — weak, distribution-style close near the session low (~7,267 vs range 7,266-7,397).

what_changed_today: DOWNLEG CONFIRMED: close 7,266.99 fully retraced the May-June advance and pierced through every Fib level (236/382/500/618) below the swing high — the active leg is fully retraced and the engine re-anchored downside to the 7,239.85 nervous zone.
Price closed BELOW the 20-day SMA / middle Bollinger band after an extended advance — a regime-shift signal, not a normal dip; price now sits only ~0.74% above the rising 50-day SMA.
VIX climbed back to ~22.2, above the 20 risk threshold and back over its 50-day MA — forced-selling risk re-elevated after the brief early-June cooldown.
Fear & Greed headline dropped into the high-20s (Fear), with multiple sub-gauges (breadth, safe-haven, junk-bond) at EXTREME FEAR — sentiment has fully rolled from greed to fear.
Monte Carlo edge is decisively bearish: 82% down-first, median 2 days to downside vs 23 days upside — fails all three regime thresholds.
Intraday tape was weak: a morning push to ~7,397 faded all day to a bottom-third close near 7,267, a textbook distribution session.
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: SPX has decisively rolled over from its early-June top: today's close fully retraced the May-June advance, sliced through every Fibonacci support, and finished in the bottom third of the range below the 20-day SMA — a confirmed regime shift, not a dip. The mechanical bull skeleton survives (50d > 200d, price still ~5.7% above the 200-day), but every supporting layer has flipped negative: ERP is at a thin 0.55%, breadth and safe-haven demand are at Extreme Fear, the junk spread re-widened, VIX is back above 20, and the Monte Carlo edge is 82% down-first with downside median of just 2 days. This is a defense-and-patience posture: protect capital, let the correction work toward the 7,240 / 50-day zone, and demand a VIX capitulation-reversal plus a 20-day reclaim before any re-entry.
open_questions: Does the 7,240 nervous-zone / rising 50-day SMA confluence hold as support, or does a clean break trigger the 35% conditional cascade toward the ~6,859 margin-call zone?
Will VIX produce a capitulation spike into the high-20s/30 and reverse (a quality re-entry signal), or does the correction stay orderly without a wash-out?
Will junk-bond spreads tighten from the re-widened ~1.35-1.45% level, or sustain wide and confirm an escalating credit warning?
Does breadth (McClellan ~970, Extreme Fear) stabilize or continue deteriorating beneath price?

rows.current_reading: RSI ~38 (neutral-low, rolling down), MFI ~28 (approaching buy watch)
Closed below 20d / middle Bollinger band after extended advance
Near/just above lower band after walking it lower
Junk spread re-widened to ~1.35-1.45%, Extreme Fear
McClellan ~970 (Extreme Fear), net new highs fading, 52wk strength Fear
Full retrace of advance; nervous zone ~7,240 in play; no capitulation yet
Trend mechanics positive; momentum, breadth, credit, sentiment, MC all negative
Defense and patience: protect capital, no re-entry until VIX capitulation-reversal + 20d reclaim

confirming_evidence: 50-day SMA (~7,213) remains above the 200-day SMA (~6,872) and price stays ~5.7% above the 200-day — the structural bull skeleton is mechanically intact.
Forward PE at 19.64x sits in the moderate 18-20x band, not an extreme valuation ceiling.
MFI cooling toward ~28 and RSI ~38 are approaching buy-watch territory, hinting the downside is becoming stretched even as confirmation is absent.
Drift path remains mildly positive (60d ~7,371), and the rally-exhaustion score is Low, so the longer-run upward drift is not structurally broken.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: 50d > 200d and price above both major SMAs keep the bull regime alive; this is a normal mid-cycle pullback toward the rising 50-day.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Close fully retraced the May-June advance through every Fib level and broke below the 20-day SMA — a regime shift after an extended advance, not a dip.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A close below the 20-day SMA after an extended advance is a regime shift, not just a normal dip.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Junk spreads remain near ~1.35% in absolute terms and have spiked-and-faded before without derailing the trend.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Junk spread re-widened to Extreme Fear and McClellan breadth sits at ~970 (Extreme Fear) — credit and breadth both diverge negatively beneath price.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not yet broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Drift path stays mildly positive and exhaustion is Low, so the longer-run upward bias is intact.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: First-hit probabilities are 82% down-first with a 2-day downside median vs 23-day upside — the near-term edge is decisively to the downside and fails all thresholds.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Never use Monte Carlo output in isolation; interpret through structural bias — but a sub-threshold, down-first edge confirms defensive posture.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: MFI ~28 / RSI ~38 and price at the lower Bollinger band suggest a bounce is near.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: No VIX capitulation spike-and-reverse, put/call only ~0.79, and breadth still deteriorating — re-entry conditions are incomplete.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A correction without a volatility spike is orderly, not capitulative; require 3 of 4 re-entry conditions before acting.

original_tool_input.primary_tension: A mechanically intact bull skeleton (50d > 200d, price still ~5.7% above the 200-day) is colliding with a confirmed active downleg — a full retracement of the May-June advance, a break below the 20-day SMA, Extreme Fear breadth and credit, VIX back above 20, and an 82% down-first Monte Carlo edge — yet the absence of a capitulation washout keeps any re-entry premature, leaving defense and patience as the only justified posture.
